fields.helpText: This is the name of the template as displayed in the CAPRI GUI.   Name is TemplateName~<patnum>   or is TemplateName~<patnum>T<testver>      where:     patnum is the patch number     testver is "Test Version" number   Examples:    JOINTS~78T1    SPINE~92
The text placed in this field will be displayed in the template selection  dialog in the CAPRI GUI to assist the user in making a selection.

If a template is loaded but should not become active until a date in the  future, that date can be set with this field.  If the date entered has  already passed, the template will become active immediately.

Placing a date in this field will de-activate the template on the date  specified.  It will often occur that an entry will contain a  de-activation date and another entry in the file that's meant to replace  it will contain the same date but in its activation date field.   Once a  template has de-activated, a user will not be able to create a new  document using that template but can still access old documents  previously created with the template.

This field contains the Windows format screen definition that will  generate the template in the CAPRI GUI.  WARNING:  DO NOT MODIFY THIS  FIELD TO PREVENT CORRUPTION OF THE TEMPLATE. If there is a problem, the field will need to be updated at a national level and re-distributed.
FACILITATES THE CUTTING OF BUILDS SINCE A SCREEN CAN CHECK FOR THOSE WHOSE 'LAST UPDATE' IS GREATER THAN THE LAST DISTRIBUTION.
If set to NO, this template will be accessible by CAPRI for merging  purposes but not selectable by the user in the forms list.
Enter the number of Windows Objects contained within the Template.
This is a value used internally by the CAPRI team to verify a template's  version prior to being distributed to the field.
